OVERRULED AS TO REQUEST REGARDING PUNITIVE DAMAGES, WHICH MAY ONLY BE CHALLENGED BY A MOTION TO STRIKE. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS ANDREW PETERSON & SUSAN PETERSON'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER DEEMING REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. PURSUANT TO CCP SEC. 2033.280, REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION NOS. 1-7 AND 20-38 ARE DEEMED ADMITTED. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION NOS. 8-19. DEFENDANT ZACH GEORGOPOULOUS TIMELY OBJECTED TO THESE RFAS, ASSERTING ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE ON BEHALF OF HIS CLIENT. (SEE UNION BANK OF CALIFORNIA, N.A. V. SUPERIOR COURT (2005) 130 CAL.APP.4TH 378, 399, "[I]N THE CASE OF THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE, ALTHOUGH THE CLIENT IS THE HOLDER OF THE PRIVILEGE, THE ATTORNEY MUST CLAIM THE PRIVILEGE UNLESS INSTRUCTED OTHERWISE BY A PERSON WITH THE POWER TO WAIVE THE PRIVILEGE.") FURTHER, PLAINTIFFS CITE NO AUTHORITY THAT SUPPORTS THEIR CONTENTION THAT THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE MAY BE WAIVED BY DEEMING REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ROARK O'NEILL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, MODIFIED TO INCLUDE THE FRAUD CAUSES OF ACTION. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED, AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE DERIVATIVE CLAIMS OF NEGLIGENCE AND BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY. DEFENDANT HAS ESTABLISHED THAT CASTRO TERMINATED THE AGENCY RELATIONSHIP WITH DEFENDANT IN JULY 2009. (UMF #21.) PLAINTIFFS HAVE FAILED TO PROVIDE ANY EVIDENCE TO CONTRADICT THIS FACT OR TO RAISE AN ISSUE REGARDING CASTRO'S AUTHORITY TO DO SO. PLAINTIFFS' OBJECTION TO DEFENDANT'S EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT IS OVERRULED. THE STATEMENT MADE BY CASTRO IS NOT HEARSAY, AS IT WAS NOT OFFERED TO PROVE THE TRUTH OF THE MATTER ASSERTED, BUT RATHER TO ESTABLISH ONLY THAT DEFENDANT HEARD IT. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE FRAUD CAUSES OF ACTION AND THE CLAIMS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THE COURT'S ORDER SUSTAINING THE DEMURRERS OF OTHER DEFENDANTS IN THIS ACTION IS INAPPLICABLE TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION STATED AGAINST DEFENDANT O'NEILL. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. ATTY. ARNOLD BERSCHLER, TEL. NO. 1-800-338-1441 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS- DEFENDANTS RITCHIE COMMERCIAL AND MARK RITCHIE;
ATTY. JOSEPH F. CHARLES OF THE PETERSON MARTIN REYNOLDS LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-399-2900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT ROARK O'NEIL.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANTs RITCHIE COMMERCIAL and MARK RITCHIE'S Motion For Good Faith Settlement Determination is denied. First, there is little point in this motion because Mr. O'Neil seeks statutory indemnity and a finding of good faith per CCP 877.6 will not bar that claim. See Kantor v. Housing Authority of Fresno County, (1992) 8 Cal.App.4th 424. Second, the moving parties do not present substantial evidence that roughly approximates plaintiff's total recovery or moving parties' proportionate liability in compliance with Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc., (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jonathan H. Finke, Esq. of Peterson Martin Reynolds LLP, T:415.399.2900, for defendant and moving party;
Martin Glickfeld, Esq. of the Law Office of Martin Glickfeld, T:415.441.7491, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT ROARK O'NEILL's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication, modified to include the fraud causes of action. Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment is denied, and Summary Adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. Summary adjudication is granted as to the derivative claims of negligence and breach of fiduciary duty. Defendant has established that Castro terminated the agency relationship with Defendant in July 2009. (UMF #21.) Plaintiffs have failed to provide any evidence to contradict this fact or to raise an issue regarding Castro's authority to do so. Plaintiffs' objection to Defendant's evidence in support is overruled. The statement made by Castro is not hearsay, as it was not offered to prove the truth of the matter asserted, but rather to establish only that Defendant heard it. Summary adjudication is denied as to the fraud causes of action and the claims for punitive damages. The Court's Order sustaining the demurrers of other defendants in this action is inapplicable to the causes of action stated against Defendant O'Neill. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

Mtn For An Order Deeming Req For Admissions
Off Calendar Sep-24-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFFs ANDREW PETERSON & SUSAN PETERSON's Motion For An Order Deeming Request For Admissions is Granted in part and denied in part. Pursuant to CCP sec. 2033.280, Requests for Admission nos. 1-7 and 20-38 are deemed admitted. The motion is denied as to Requests for Admission nos. 8-19. Defendant Zach Georgopoulous timely objected to these RFAs, asserting attorney-client privilege on behalf of his client. (See Union Bank of California, N.A. v. Superior Court (2005) 130 Cal.App.4th 378, 399, "[I]n the case of the attorney-client privilege, although the client is the holder of the privilege, the attorney must claim the privilege unless instructed otherwise by a person with the power to waive the privilege.") Further, Plaintiffs cite no authority that supports their contention that the attorney-client privilege may be waived by deeming Requests for Admissions admitted. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
